description_long: This object is a vintage-style electronic device, likely a radio transceiver or communications receiver, featuring a rectangular, boxy body with a dark, matte exterior. The front panel is equipped with a large analog meter, several rotary knobs, toggle switches, and a pair of dials, all set in a metallic faceplate. A wired, gooseneck microphone with a weighted base is attached to the side, suggesting the device is used for audio communication. The back and sides are plain, with the only notable feature being the cable connecting the microphone to the main unit. The device's construction appears robust, with a metal chassis and plastic or Bakelite knobs. This type of equipment would typically be found in urban settings such as garages, workshops, or amateur radio operator stations.
description: A vintage electronic communication device with a dark boxy body, metallic front panel, analog meter, and attached gooseneck microphone.
description_view_0: The top and one side of the dark, rectangular box are visible, with the microphone and its cable protruding from the right.
description_view_1: A plain side view of the box, showing the microphone and its cable attached to the rear right corner.
description_view_2: The front panel displays an analog meter, multiple knobs, switches, and dials, with the microphone and cable extending from the left.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the box is shown, with the microphone and cable visible on the left, and no other features present.
attribute_control_interface: rotary dial, physical buttons, toggle switches, microphone
